rationale: All four acceptance criteria pass. The implementation adds a 4-line guard in goBackFromWorkflowEdit — consistent with the reference pattern in goBackFromWorkflowExecutions — and a new test file with 2 test cases. The direct-link test (AC1/AC2) directly exercises the new guard; the in-app navigation test (AC3) confirms no regression. Commit messages and branch naming conform to project conventions. License headers present. Security: no concerns. Code quality guide absent (no violations detectable in the 4-line production change). Three deferred test quality items noted: (a) the in-app nav test cannot distinguish which internal branch fired since both produce identical observable router.push output with the test's history config; (b) no test for single-entry (currentIndex=0) or all-same-workflow-ID history stacks; (c) history is a shared proxy singleton — Vitest per-file isolation should guard against state bleed but is undocumented. None of these is blocking for this XS change.
follow_ups: EC-001: Strengthen the in-app nav test by spying on goBackWorkflows to assert it receives the WOKRFLOW_EXECUTIONS argument, distinguishing the two internal branches regardless of history stack content.
EC-003/EC-004: Add tests for single-entry stack (currentIndex=0) and all-same-workflow-ID stack to verify the !safeRoute guard fires correctly in those cases.
EC-005: Document or verify Vitest per-file module isolation guarantees for the history proxy singleton across test files.

business_review.criterion: AC1: When the workflow edit page is opened from a direct link, clicking Back navigates to the workflow list.
business_review.notes: With empty history stack (currentIndex=-1), findFirstNonWorkflowRoute returns null. New !safeRoute guard fires and calls goBackWorkflows() with no args, which defaults to WORKFLOWS_ALL. Directly exercised by test case 1.

business_review.criterion: AC2: When the workflow edit page is opened from a direct link, clicking Cancel navigates to the workflow list.
business_review.notes: EditWorkflowPage Cancel calls onBack which calls goBackFromWorkflowEdit. No component changes required — the utility fix covers both Back and Cancel.

business_review.criterion: AC3: When the page is opened through in-app navigation (non-empty history stack), Back and Cancel continue to navigate to the previous valid route.
business_review.notes: When safeRoute is non-null and not NEW_WORKFLOW, both guards are skipped and the original goBackWorkflows({ name: WOKRFLOW_EXECUTIONS, ... }) call fires. navigateBack finds the prior allowed route in history and pushes to it. Exercised by test case 2.

business_review.criterion: AC4: No regression on workflow save, Save and Run, or any other navigation flow.
business_review.notes: The guard is additive. All pre-existing paths (safeRoute === NEW_WORKFLOW, truthy safeRoute) are unchanged. No modifications to save/run handlers, router.tsx, useHistoryStack.tsx, or shared utilities.

standards_review.notes: Both commits conform: 'EPMCDME-13308: Fix Back and Cancel navigation on direct-link workflow edit page' and 'EPMCDME-13308: Add regression test for in-app navigation path'.
Branch EPMCDME-13308_fix-workflow-back-cancel-direct-link conforms to the documented pattern.
New test file includes the required Apache 2.0 copyright header for 2026 EPAM Systems.
No security concerns. Pure navigation utility — no auth, no secrets, no injection vectors.
Guide file not present in repository. No code quality violations detectable in the 4-line production change.
